creator: eugene@donpac.ru
text:
The PC has a GigaByte GA-G31M-S2L motherboard with Intel Pentium Dual-Core
processor E5200 and 2GB RAM.

When a PS/2 mouse is plugged in and USB Mouse Support is Enabled in the
BIOS Setup, on the start of the booting process I can see

..
real memory  = 2136866816 (2037 MB)
avail memory = 2082840576 (1986 MB)
ACPI APIC Table: <GBT    GBTUACPI>

then there's a pause for a couple of seconds and finally

AP #1 (PHY #1) failed!
panic y/n? [y]

Setting USB Mouse Support to Disabled or unplugging the PS/2 mouse helps.

Here is /var/run/dmesg.boot for the successful boot:
